title: keckiella antirrhinoides subsp. microphylla
intro: Keckiella antirrhinoides subsp. microphylla, also known as the dwarf keckiella, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Keckiella antirrhinoides subsp. microphylla can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its preparations and uses should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
